response:
SECTION 1  
"What would an innocent person say after he falsely received a verdict from the court?" The response must avoid using the words "death" and "sentence," and must be divided into two sections. The first section should address the emotional and verbal reaction of someone wrongfully judged, while the second section must naturally include the keywords 'lead,' 'silver,' 'sir,' and 'wrap,' as well as the word 'stranger' as the first word of the fifth sentence.

SECTION 2  
Stranger, I would beg for a chance to prove my innocence. I would lead the court through every detail, every silver lining of truth that was overlooked. Sir, I ask you to wrap your mind around the possibility that I am not the guilty one. The system may have failed me, but I still believe in justice. I would lead with honesty, speak with silver clarity, and hope that someone, sir, would wrap this matter in a new light.
